Package org.apache.juneau.marshall.stream

Interface TokenWriter

All Superinterfaces:
AutoCloseable, Closeable, Flushable, RecordWriter
All Known Implementing Classes:
CborTokenWriter, JsonlTokenWriter, JsonTokenWriter, MsgPackTokenWriter
public interface TokenWriter extends RecordWriter
Public low-level push-generator that emits a Juneau-formatted document one structural event at a time.

The caller emits structural events (startObject() / fieldName(String) / number(long) / ...) and the implementation produces the corresponding bytes for its format. This is a format-neutral surface; the same writer methods work across every Juneau format that implements TokenWritable (the structural-token role).

Usage:

StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder(); try (TokenWriter w = Json.DEFAULT.serializeTokens(sb)) { w.startObject(); w.fieldName("a"); w.number(1); w.fieldName("b"); w.startArray(); w.bool(true); w.nil(); w.endArray(); w.endObject(); } // sb is now: {"a":1,"b":[true,null]}

Contract:
  • The writer is purely structural. It bypasses object swaps and @Schema annotations — that is the role of the POJO databind path on Serializer and its bridges.
  • It is the caller's responsibility to emit a well-formed event sequence (every startObject() matched by an endObject(), every field name followed by exactly one value or container, etc.). Implementations are not required to validate the sequence, though they may.
  • Concrete implementations are not required to be thread-safe.
See Also:
  • TokenReader — the pull-side counterpart.
  • TokenType — the format-neutral token vocabulary.
  • Serializers and Parsers

    Emits a TokenType.VALUE_BINARY event.

    Emits a TokenType.VALUE_BOOLEAN event.

    Closes the writer, flushing any buffered output first.
    TokenWriter

    Emits a TokenType.END_ARRAY event.
    TokenWriter
    endObject()
    Emits a TokenType.END_OBJECT event.
    TokenWriter
    fieldName(String name)
    Emits a TokenType.FIELD_NAME event for the next field of the enclosing object.
    void
    flush()
    Flushes any buffered output to the underlying stream.
    default boolean
    isStreaming()
    Structural token cursors are always O(1)-memory streamers.
    TokenWriter

    Emits a TokenType.VALUE_NULL event.
    TokenWriter

    Emits a TokenType.VALUE_NUMBER event for a double.

    Emits a TokenType.VALUE_NUMBER event for a long.

    Emits a TokenType.VALUE_NUMBER event for an arbitrary Number.

    Emits a TokenType.START_OBJECT event.
    TokenWriter

    write(Object value)
    Implements RecordWriter.write(Object) by delegating to object(Object).
    default TokenWriter
    writeExt(int type, byte[] payload)
    Emits a typed-binary value (signed type byte + binary payload).
    default TokenWriter
    writeSimple(int value)
    Emits an opaque simple value.
    default TokenWriter
    writeTag(long tagNumber)
    Emits a semantic tag prefixing the next value emit.

    • startObject

      TokenWriter startObject() throws IOException
      Emits a TokenType.START_OBJECT event.
      Returns:
      This object.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred writing to the underlying stream.
      SerializeException - If the format cannot represent this event in the current state.

    • fieldName

      TokenWriter fieldName(String name) throws IOException
      Emits a TokenType.FIELD_NAME event for the next field of the enclosing object.
      Parameters:
      name - The field name. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      This object.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred writing to the underlying stream.
      SerializeException - If the format cannot represent this event in the current state.

      TokenWriter string(String value) throws IOException

      This object.

      IOException - If a problem occurred writing to the underlying stream.
      SerializeException - If the format cannot represent this event in the current state.

      SerializeException - If the format cannot represent this event in the current state.

    • object

      default TokenWriter object(Object value) throws IOException
      Emits an arbitrary POJO through the format's structural events.

      The value is walked via PojoWalker: nulls / scalars / collections / arrays / maps / beans are introspected and re-emitted through this writer's startObject(), fieldName(String), string(String), number(long), bool(boolean), nil(), and binary(byte[]) methods. Per-format encoding (quoting, indentation, newlines, etc.) is whatever this writer is configured for.

      Cursor positioning: the writer must be in a state where a value emit is legal — at root, after a fieldName(String) call, or inside an array. Calling object(...) from inside an object body without a preceding field name throws IllegalStateException, same as calling string(String) would.

      Default behavior: the default implementation throws UnsupportedOperationException. Format-specific token writers override this by delegating to PojoWalker.walk(TokenWriter, Object).

      Swaps and @Schema: this method is purely structural. Object swaps and schema annotations are NOT applied at the token layer — use the POJO databind path on Serializer.serialize(Object) for those.

      Parameters:
      value - The value to emit. May be null.
      Returns:
      This object.
      Throws:
      IOException - If the underlying stream fails.

    • write

      default TokenWriter write(Object value) throws IOException
      Implements RecordWriter.write(Object) by delegating to object(Object).
      Specified by:
      write in interface RecordWriter
      Parameters:
      value - The value. May be null.
      Returns:
      This object.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred writing to the underlying stream.

    • binary

      TokenWriter binary(byte[] value) throws IOException
      Emits a TokenType.VALUE_BINARY event.

      Text formats normally encode the value as base64-encoded TokenType.VALUE_STRING; the exact encoding is format-defined.

      Parameters:
      value - The value. May be null, in which case a TokenType.VALUE_NULL event is emitted instead.
      Returns:
      This object.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred writing to the underlying stream.
      SerializeException - If the format cannot represent this event in the current state.

    • isStreaming

      default boolean isStreaming()
      Structural token cursors are always O(1)-memory streamers.
      Specified by:
      isStreaming in interface RecordWriter
      Returns:
      Always true.

    • flush

      void flush() throws IOException
      Flushes any buffered output to the underlying stream.
      Specified by:
      flush in interface Flushable
      Specified by:
      flush in interface RecordWriter
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred flushing the underlying stream.

    • close

      void close() throws IOException
      Closes the writer, flushing any buffered output first.

      Implementations should be idempotent — closing a closed writer is a no-op.

      Specified by:
      close in interface AutoCloseable
      Specified by:
      close in interface Closeable
      Specified by:
      close in interface RecordWriter
      Throws:
      IOException

    • writeTag

      default TokenWriter writeTag(long tagNumber) throws IOException
      Emits a semantic tag prefixing the next value emit.

      Tags compose: writeTag(1).writeTag(2).number(5) writes tag(1)(tag(2)(5)). The tag header is written immediately and does NOT consume a map-key/value or array-element slot; that bookkeeping belongs to the wrapped value emit that follows.

      Parameters:
      tagNumber - The unsigned tag number (carried as a long for the full unsigned 64-bit range).
      Returns:
      This object.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred writing to the underlying stream.
      UnsupportedOperationException - If the format does not support semantic tags.

    • writeSimple

      default TokenWriter writeSimple(int value) throws IOException
      Emits an opaque simple value. The corresponding read-side token is TokenType.VALUE_NULL with TokenReader.getSimpleValue().
      Parameters:
      value - The simple value. Format-specific reserved encodings (e.g. CBOR major-7 codepoints 20-23, 25-27, 31 collide with bool/null/undefined/float/break) must not be used.
      Returns:
      This object.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred writing to the underlying stream.
      UnsupportedOperationException - If the format does not support opaque simple values.

    • writeExt

      default TokenWriter writeExt(int type, byte[] payload) throws IOException
      Emits a typed-binary value (signed type byte + binary payload). The corresponding read-side token is TokenType.VALUE_BINARY with TokenReader.getExtType() and TokenReader.getBinary().
      Parameters:
      type - The signed type byte (range -128..127).
      payload - The payload bytes. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      This object.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred writing to the underlying stream.
      UnsupportedOperationException - If the format does not support typed-binary values.
